title: SPDX identifiers and license expressions
description: SPDX gives each license a stable identifier and a small grammar (AND/OR/WITH) for expressing combined and exception-bearing terms.
body: Use when recording or parsing the license of a component. An SPDX identifier (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-or-later) is a stable string from the SPDX License List. Expressions combine them: OR means the recipient may choose a disjunct (dual licensing), AND means all terms apply cumulatively, and WITH attaches an exception (GPL-2.0-with-classpath-exception relaxes linking). The '+' / '-or-later' suffix lets a later version of the license apply. Do not invent identifiers or treat 'GPL' as unambiguous about version. Actions: validate identifiers against the current list, record the chosen disjunct for OR, and treat deprecated identifiers (e.g. GPL-2.0 vs GPL-2.0-only) as fixups. Validation signals include every inventory identifier parsing against the current SPDX list. Risk boundary: an unparseable or deprecated identifier means the obligation is undetermined; escalate.
